stories.title: Flattery and fanfare as Trump welcomed to China - but thorny issues remain
stories.body: The leaders of the world's two superpowers were all smiles for the cameras during the US president's visit to Beijing.

stories.title: 'Floating armoury' ship reportedly seized by Iran
stories.body: A vessel said to be a “floating armoury” in the Gulf of Oman has been seized by Iranian military personnel, according to reports.

stories.title: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble of Kyiv flats after massive Russian strikes
stories.body: Two children are among at least 12 killed in Ukraine's capital in massive Russian drone and missile attacks, officials say.

stories.title: Giant new dinosaur identified from fossils in Thailand
stories.body: The nagatitan is the largest dinosaur found in South-East Asia and weighs as much as nine elephants.

stories.title: Cuba tells US to lift blockade instead of offering aid as energy crisis worsens
stories.body: The Communist-run island has seen rare protests over worsening power cuts due to shortages of fuel.

stories.title: Latvian PM resigns after row over stray Ukrainian drones
stories.body: Drones bound for Russia crashed down in Latvia last week, prompting a political fallout.

stories.title: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows
stories.body: As El Niño develops this year, scientists are increasingly confident it could be one of the strongest on record with global consequences, as Simon King explains.

stories.title: 'We didn't die': Pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic with 10 aboard
stories.body: The 11 survivors spent roughly five hours on a life raft before being rescued by the US Air Force off Florida coast.

stories.title: War criminal Ratko Mladić's jail release request denied
stories.body: A UN judge admits the 84-year-old is "in the final stages of his life", but says prison conditions in The Hague ensure his maximum comfort.

stories.title: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
stories.body: Former Power Minister Saleh Mamman was convicted last week - but the authorities do not know his whereabouts.

stories.title: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
stories.body: Rebel fighters and Rwandan troops are accused of committing atrocities after capturing the DR Congo city of Uvira in December.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 22 people, health ministry says
stories.body: Eight children are among those reported dead after a series of air strikes hit the south of Beirut.

stories.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
stories.body: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

stories.title: Gunshots fired in Philippine Senate where lawmaker wanted by ICC is holed up
stories.body: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, a former police chief, enforced former President Rodrigo Duterte's deadly drug war.

stories.title: Woman isolating on British island in South Pacific after hantavirus contact
stories.body: The woman who was on a virus-hit ship shows "no signs of illness", the government of Pitcairn Islands, a British Overseas Territory, says.

stories.title: Dozens of dogs rescued and suspect arrested in Uganda after BBC investigation
stories.body: A BBC investigation exposed how scammers posted social content of dogs in distress to dupe donors into giving money.

stories.title: Epstein abused me while under house arrest, survivor tells US lawmakers
stories.body: Roza was among several victims who gave testimony to Democratic lawmakers on Tuesday.

stories.title: Shrinking Milka chocolate bar tricked consumers, says German court
stories.body: A court in Bremen has found the manufacturer of the classic Alpine Milk chocolate bar guilty of "shrinkflation".

stories.title: Passengers allowed to leave norovirus-hit cruise ship
stories.body: Passengers on the ship showing no symptoms are allowed to leave, authorities say, after 49 people fell ill from gastrointestinal sickness.

stories.title: What to know as Trump visits Xi in China
stories.body: Trump has landed in Beijing for the first visit by a US leader since his own in 2017.

narrative:
🟡 **Mood: Cautious**  
Today's signals reflect a cautious optimism, with several positive developments in governance and stability, but lingering contested dynamics and unresolved tensions temper the outlook.

**1. Governance and stability-oriented signals dominate today's structural pattern.** The sentencing of a former Nigerian minister to 75 years in prison for corruption marks a rare and significant accountability milestone in a region often plagued by impunity. Similarly, the denial of war criminal Ratko Mladić's jail release request reinforces institutional resolve in addressing historical atrocities. These signals suggest incremental but meaningful progress in governance-oriented trajectories, particularly in regions where stability has been fragile. While these actions are positive, they remain isolated events rather than systemic shifts, underscoring the need for sustained institutional reforms.

**2. Care-oriented signals highlight human resilience and intervention.** The rescue of dozens of dogs in Uganda following a BBC investigation and the survival of passengers in a dramatic Atlantic crash landing reflect moments of care and collective action. These narratives, while smaller in scale, underscore the importance of human agency in crisis scenarios. They also serve as reminders of the broader societal value placed on intervention and recovery, even amid challenging circumstances. However, these signals remain anecdotal and do not point to broader structural changes.

**3. Contested signals reveal underlying tensions in global dynamics.** The visit of former U.S. President Trump to China, while framed positively, carries significant contested undertones. The accompanying supporting signal about unresolved "thorny issues" highlights the fragility of U.S.-China relations, particularly in the context of trade, security, and geopolitical competition. Similarly, the seizure of a "floating armoury" ship by Iran introduces new uncertainties in the Gulf region, underscoring the persistent volatility in global security dynamics. These contested signals suggest that while diplomatic engagements and enforcement actions are occurring, the underlying tensions remain unresolved.

**4. Longer-term trajectories point to structural fragility in governance and climate.** Signals such as the resignation of Latvia's prime minister over stray Ukrainian drones and warnings of record global temperatures due to a potential strong El Niño highlight vulnerabilities in governance and environmental systems. These events, while seemingly disparate, reflect broader structural challenges: the strain on leadership amid geopolitical spillovers and the worsening impact of climate change on global stability. Both trajectories demand sustained attention as they are likely to exacerbate existing pressures on global institutions.

**What to watch next:**  
- **Governance accountability:** Monitor whether the Nigerian corruption verdict triggers broader anti-corruption momentum in the region.  
- **Geopolitical flashpoints:** Watch for developments in U.S.-China relations post-Trump's visit and any escalations tied to Iran's seizure of the "floating armoury."  
- **Climate impact:** Pay close attention to updates on El Niño forecasts and their implications for global food security and disaster preparedness.  

Today's signals suggest cautious progress in some areas but underscore the need for vigilance in addressing contested and fragile dynamics.
